Approve media schedules, budget allocations, and media buying decisions prior to execution. Ensure reporting quality, providing accurate and timely campaign reports, monthly reviews, and post-campaign analyses (PCA). Proactively propose data-driven solutions to enhance performance (A/B testing, creative optimization, bidding strategies, etc.). 3. Client Management & Business Development Maintain strong relationships with clients at manager/senior manager level, acting as a trusted strategic partner. Identify and leverage upsell/cross-sell opportunities to expand business scope. Participate in new business pitches, prepare proposals, and present media solutions to potential clients. 4. Team Management & Development Lead, coach, and mentor a team of Media Planners/Senior Planners (2–5 members). Assign tasks, review outputs, and ensure continuous capability development within the team. Foster a data-driven, proactive, and creative team culture. 5. Internal & Partner Collaboration Collaborate closely with Creative, Analytics, Social, and Account teams to ensure seamless strategy integration. Manage relationships with platform partners (Google, Meta, TikTok, Zalo) to stay updated on new products and optimize partnership opportunities.

Excellent presentation and communication skills in Vietnamese; proficiency in English is a plus. Strong analytical skills, with proficiency in Excel/Google Sheets; experience with GA4 and Looker Studio is a plus. Leadership capabilities with the ability to motivate and develop team members. DV360 / Google Marketing Platform Certification. Job Title: [Factory 1] DQCO - Cleaning, Disinfection & Contamination Control Officer (Pharmacy/ Biotech/ Chemical)
Company: CÔNG TY TNHH ROHTO-MENTHOLATUM (VIỆT NAM)
Job Description: The DQCO performs independent, real-time oversight of all GMP activities at the facility, without direct involvement in production or routine quality release (QA release / QC). The role ensures that aseptic compliance, data integrity, environmental monitoring, and contamination control are maintained to the highest regulatory standards across all production shifts. Cleaning, Disinfection & Contamination Control (CCS Oversight) Execution Oversight: Oversee execution of cleaning and disinfection procedures against approved SOPs. Verify disinfectant contact times and confirm disinfection effectiveness. Ensure disinfectant rotation is performed correctly and on schedule. Audit cleaning records versus actual practices (including CCTV verification). Contamination Control: Ensure adherence to the Contamination Control Strategy (CCS) across all operations. Evaluate cleaning validation results and assess residue risk. Identify gaps in cleaning effectiveness and recommend improvements. Immediately escalate cleaning failures and recommend production hold when necessary.
Job Requirements: Education Bachelor's degree in Pharmacy, Microbiology, Chemistry, Biotechnology, Chemical Engineering, or a related science discipline. Postgraduate qualification or specialised GMP certification is an advantage. Work Experience Experience in a GMP environment; sterile manufacturing experience is strongly preferred. Experience in Aseptic oversight, Environmental Monitoring, or QA/QC roles is a significant advantage. Technical Knowledge cGMP: 21 CFR Part 210/211, EU GMP, WHO GMP. Data Integrity – ALCOA+ (FDA, MHRA, WHO Guidance). Aseptic Process Simulation (Media Fill). ICH Q9 (Quality Risk Management) and root cause investigation tools (RCA, 5-Why, Fishbone). Skills Detail observation skills, accurate and contemporaneous documentation. Audit skills, effective questioning, and risk assessment. Written reporting skills in both Vietnamese and English. Teamwork and cross-departmental communication skills. Proficient in Microsoft Office; Power BI / advanced Excel is an advantage. Personal Attributes High integrity, independent judgment, unwavering under production schedule pressure. Meticulous, critical thinking, and strong compliance mindset. Willing to work shift-based schedules including nights and weekends as required.

Company: Công Ty TNHH CJ Foods Việt Nam
Job Description: Supply Planner is responsible for developing and maintaining an optimized supply plan that ensures product availability while balancing capacity, inventory, and service level objectives. This role plays a key part in translating the demand plan into actionable production and replenishment schedules, coordinating closely with production, procurement, logistics, and demand planning teams. 1. Supply Planning Execution • Develop weekly and monthly supply plans based on the approved demand forecast. • Create feasible Master Production Schedules (MPS) by balancing demand, capacity, and material availability. • Review production constraints and propose adjustment plans or alternative sourcing options. • Monitor plan adherence and recommend corrective actions for supply gaps. 2. Inventory & Service Control • Maintain appropriate stock levels to achieve service level targets while minimizing excess inventory. • Monitor and control inventory by SKU and location based on safety stock and days of inventory targets. • Identify risks of stock-out or overstock and propose mitigation actions. • Support analysis for slow-moving and obsolete stock management. 3. Cross-functional Collaboration • Coordinate with Demand Planning to review forecast changes and align on supply scenarios. • Work with Production to confirm schedule feasibility and capacity utilization. • Liaise with Procurement for raw material readiness and replenishment planning. • Collaborate with Logistics and Customer Service to ensure on-time delivery to customers. 4. Reporting & Process Improvement • Prepare weekly and monthly reports on production plan, supply performance, and service level. • Support S&OP (Sales & Operations Planning) process with supply input and recovery actions. • Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives in supply planning and system utilization. • Maintain planning data accuracy in ERP/MRP systems (SAP or equivalent).
Job Requirements: Qualifications Education & Experience • Bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain Management, Industrial Engineering, or Business Administration. • 2–4 years of experience in supply planning, production planning, or related fields. • Experience in FMCG / Food Manufacturing / Logistics preferred. Technical Skills • Familiar with ERP/MRP systems (SAP preferred). • Proficient in Microsoft Excel (Pivot, Power Query); data visualization tools (Power BI, Tableau) is a plus. • Knowledge of capacity planning, inventory management, and S&OP concepts. Soft Skills • Analytical and detail-oriented with strong problem-solving skills. • Good communication and cross-functional collaboration skills. • Proactive mindset with ability to manage priorities and deadlines. • High sense of ownership and continuous improvement attitude.

This role involves identifying and onboarding suitable KOLs/KOCs, managing end-to-end affiliate campaigns, and maintaining strong relationships with creators and MCNs. You will collaborate closely with internal teams to ensure seamless execution of campaigns, negotiate deals, track performance, and generate insights to optimize ROI. The ideal candidate is a proactive and data-driven marketer who understands social media trends, enjoys working with creators, and thrives in a fast-paced, collaborative environment. . Build and maintain strong relationships with affiliates to develop a solid network, and collaborate with MCNs to source potential creators for affiliate bookings. Negotiate pricing and contract terms with KOLs/KOCs, onboard them for collaborations, and manage affiliate commissions while ensuring clear alignment on timelines and ways of working. Brief KOLs/KOCs on scripts and brand expectations, and manage them throughout the campaign to ensure effective, on-time content delivery that meets brand standards. Track and analyze affiliate performance to generate actionable insights that drive better results and optimize ROI. Identify trends, opportunities, and root causes behind performance fluctuations to continuously improve affiliate strategies.

Excellent negotiation, relationship management, and campaign planning skills, with strong analytical ability to assess performance metrics. Friendly, outgoing, and collaborative personality with strong communication skills. Active TikTok user who stays updated on the latest trends and creator landscape. Data-driven mindset with great attention to detail and focus on performance outcomes. Ability to work both independently and as part of a team, and to perform well under pressure.

With over 4,500 employees, the company is committed to performance, diversity, and talent development. Its ‘ Choices for Change’ strategy targets long-term impact by 2030, focusing on prosperous farmers, thriving communities, climate action, and sustainability. JOB IN SUMMARY The Deputy HR Manager supports the HR Manager in overseeing the full spectrum of Human Resources operations. This role ensures HR policies, procedures, and programs are implemented effectively across the organization, while also leading key HR initiatives related to recruitment, employee relations, performance management, training and development, HR compliance, and administration functions. Ensure HR standards comply with local labor laws and company regulations. Updating new labor law to adapt company’s polices. Handle HR audits (BRC, Social audits & internal audits). 2) Recruitment & Staffing Ensure timely recruitment to meet company requirements. Act as HR Manager during periods of absence. Coach and develop junior HR staff to enhance capability.

Company: CÔNG TY TNHH SKYLAB INNOGRAM VIỆT NAM
Job Description: Why this role is different Most engineering jobs ask you to maintain legacy systems or add features to crowded codebases. This isn't that. You'll be one of the founding engineers shaping two products that sit at the intersection of AI, enterprise software, and infrastructure — at a moment when those three things are being rewritten from scratch. The code you write in your first six months will still be running in production five years from now. The architectural decisions you make will shape how thousands of Korean small businesses experience AI, and how GPU compute is delivered across Southeast Asia. We don't believe in 200-person engineering orgs where you ship one feature per quarter. We believe in small teams of strong engineers who own entire systems end-to-end, ship fast, and use AI tools to operate like teams 5x their size. The two products you'll build CoSAP — AI-Powered Business Intelligence for Korean SMEs Korean small and medium businesses run on Douzone and ECount — ERP systems that hold decades of accounting and operational data, but expose it through forms and reports built for accountants, not founders. CoSAP changes that. We let business owners ask questions in plain Korean — "왜 이번 달 마진이 떨어졌어?" (why did our margin drop this month?) — and get answers backed by their actual financial data. Under the hood: a multi-tenant data layer connecting Douzone and ECount, retrieval over financial and operational data using PostgreSQL with pgvector and Qdrant, event streaming with Kafka, workflow orchestration with Kestra, and self-hosted LLM serving with vLLM. The Korean SME market is 7+ million businesses. Almost none of them have access to real business intelligence today. Fusionflow — GPU & AI Infrastructure Orchestration GPUs are the most expensive, most contested compute resource on earth right now. We operate Kubernetes-based GPU clusters across multiple data centers and turn them into reliable, multi-tenant compute that customers can actually use. Fusionflow is the orchestration layer: scheduling, isolation, observability, and operations. You'll work on real distributed systems problems — GPU scheduling under contention, node health and failure recovery, network performance tuning across InfiniBand and RoCE, tenant isolation, and the operational tooling needed to run hundreds of GPUs reliably. We currently operate a 19-node K3s cluster with 160+ GPUs and are scaling significantly through 2026. What you'll actually do Concretely — not buzzwords, but the kind of tickets you'll close in your first year: • Build the next-generation ERP connector layer for CoSAP — designing schemas, sync logic, and conflict resolution for ECount and Douzone data flowing into our analytics layer. • Improve retrieval quality for natural-language financial queries — chunking strategies, embedding models, hybrid search, and evaluation pipelines. • Design and operate Fusionflow's GPU scheduling logic — how do we fairly allocate scarce GPU resources across tenants while keeping latency low? • Harden cluster operations — node health monitoring, automated remediation, network tuning, DDoS mitigation, observability. • Write production-grade APIs and internal tooling in Python and TypeScript that other engineers, ops staff, and customers depend on. • Review AI-generated code with the same rigor as human code — catching subtle bugs, hallucinated APIs, security issues, and performance regressions. • Be on-call for systems you build. Debug real outages. Write the postmortem. Ship the fix. Who we're looking for In the AI era, raw coding skill is no longer the bottleneck. Almost anyone can produce working code with Claude Code or Cursor. What separates strong engineers from average ones now is judgment, taste, and verification discipline. Here's what we actually screen for, in priority order: 1. Verification instinct — the ability to spot when AI output is wrong This is the #1 filter. AI tools produce confident, plausible-looking code that is sometimes subtly broken — hallucinated APIs, wrong async behavior, race conditions, security holes, schema mismatches. The best engineers today treat AI output as a draft from a fast, overconfident junior, not as truth. We will test this in the interview. 2. Strong fundamentals in distributed systems You understand consistency, idempotency, retries, queues, timeouts, and what happens when networks partition. You've worked with Kubernetes, Kafka (or equivalent), and PostgreSQL in production — not just in tutorials. You know why SELECT FOR UPDATE exists and when to use it. 3. Problem decomposition over prompt engineering Given an ambiguous request, you ask the right clarifying questions before writing code. You can break "sync ECount data into our analytics layer" into a concrete technical plan covering schema, ingestion, idempotency, error handling, and observability — before opening Claude Code. 4. Production ownership and operational maturity You've been on-call. You've debugged a real outage at 2 AM. You read logs, traces, and metrics fluently. You write code defensively because you've been burned. You don't say "works on my machine" — you say "let me check what differs in production." 5. Code reading > code writing Most of your time is spent reading and reviewing, not greenfield writing. You can drop into an unfamiliar codebase, orient yourself in a few hours, and identify what to change. You're comfortable navigating large repos, reading other people's code, and understanding intent. 6. AI tool fluency with healthy skepticism Daily use of Claude Code, Cursor, or Copilot. Familiarity with MCP, agent workflows, tool calling, and prompt design. Most importantly: you know where these tools fail and you compensate. A candidate who blindly trusts AI output is more dangerous than one who doesn't use it at all. 7. Clear written communication in English PR descriptions, technical specs, runbooks, prompt design, async messaging with a globally distributed team. Clear writing equals clear thinking. We work asynchronously across Singapore, HCMC, and Korea — written communication is the medium
Job Requirements: Nice-to-haves (not required): • Korean language reading ability — significant advantage for CoSAP work • Experience with ERP systems (Douzone, ECount, SAP, Oracle) • GPU/CUDA familiarity, or experience operating Kubernetes with GPU workloads • RAG and vector search tuning experience • Smart contract or blockchain experience (we have adjacent projects in tokenized infrastructure) • Open-source contributions, technical blog, or side projects we can look at. What you get from joining: Compensation & equity • Competitive base salary benchmarked against top HCMC tech employers (Grab, Shopee, VNG, Tiki) • Performance-based bonuses tied to milestones, not vague "culture fit" reviews • Meaningful equity participation — you own a piece of what you build • Health insurance for you and immediate family Growth & learning • Direct mentorship from the CTO and senior engineers across Singapore and Korea • Annual learning budget for courses, conferences, and certifications • Paid AI tool subscriptions (Claude Pro/Max, Cursor, etc.) — we want you using the best tools • Conference travel — APAC tech conferences, KubeCon, GTC, etc. • Travel to Singapore and Korea for offsites, customer visits, and team weeks. The stack you'll work with: • Languages: Python, TypeScript / Node.js (some Go for infrastructure) • Data: PostgreSQL, pgvector, Qdrant, Kafka, Kestra • AI / ML: vLLM, sentence-transformers, embedding models, RAG pipelines • Infrastructure: Kubernetes (K3s), Docker, Linux, GPU operators • Networking: InfiniBand, RoCE, DWDM (you'll learn what you don't know) • AI Tooling: Claude Code, Cursor, MCP servers, custom agent workflows • Observability: Prometheus, Grafana, structured logging • Source / CI: Git, GitHub Actions, code review culture • Location: Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam (On-site) • Level: Mid-level (3–5 years experience) • Language: English required · Korean a strong plus • Compensation: Competitive base + performance bonus + equity participation • Products: CoSAP (AI ERP for Korean SMEs) + Fusionflow (GPU orchestration)

Job Description: JOB PURPOSES: The Executive Assistant, Commercial Hub plays a key support role in enabling the effective operation of the Commercial Hub and its senior leadership team. This is not a traditional Personal Assistant role. The position goes beyond supporting one executive and is designed to provide high-quality executive, administrative, reporting, coordination, translation, and productivity support across the Commercial Hub. The role will work closely with the Deputy CEO & Head of Commercial and other senior leaders within the Commercial team to ensure smooth communication, effective meeting management, timely reporting, disciplined follow-up, and well-prepared leadership materials. The ideal candidate is highly organized, proactive, discreet, bilingual in English and Vietnamese, comfortable working with data and technology, and able to support multiple senior stakeholders in a fast-paced commercial environment. PRINCIPAL ACCOUNTABILITIES: 1. Executive and Administrative Support Provide day-to-day executive and administrative support to the Head of Commercial and selected senior leaders within the Commercial Hub. Manage calendars, meeting schedules, appointments, and business priorities. Coordinate internal and external meetings, ensuring participants, materials, and logistics are well prepared. Support business correspondence, follow-ups, and communication on behalf of senior leaders when required. Prepare meeting minutes, action points, and follow-up trackers. Support travel arrangements, business trip logistics, and related documentation when needed. Manage expense claims, payment requests, and administrative submissions in line with company policies. Handle confidential information with a high level of discretion and professionalism. 2. Commercial Reporting and Leadership Briefing Support the Commercial Hub in preparing regular reports, business updates, and leadership briefing materials. Pull, compile, and consolidate commercial data from CRM systems, internal tracking tools, and other business platforms. Prepare regular commercial reports, dashboards, and summaries for the Commercial leadership team, and ExCom. Assist in preparing briefing documents, presentation materials, and discussion notes for leadership meetings. Follow up with relevant stakeholders to collect inputs, validate information, and ensure reporting accuracy. Track key commercial actions, deadlines, and follow-up items across business units and stakeholders. Support the Commercial Hub in maintaining organized documentation and reporting archives. 3. Meeting, Workshop, and Governance Coordination Coordinate recurring commercial meetings, leadership discussions, workshops, and ad hoc business sessions. Prepare meeting agendas in coordination with relevant stakeholders. Arrange logistics for meetings, workshops, business reviews, and cross-functional discussions. Take clear and structured meeting minutes, including key decisions, action items, owners, and deadlines. Track progress of agreed actions and follow up with responsible parties. Support preparation of workshop materials, pre-read documents, and post-meeting summaries. Ensure recurring team meetings are well organized, disciplined, and outcome-focused. 4. English/Vietnamese Translation and Communication Support Provide bilingual support for both spoken and written communication. This is a core requirement of the role. Translate business documents, meeting materials, emails, reports, and presentations between English and Vietnamese. Provide bilingual support during meetings, discussions, and workshops when required. Help ensure messages are communicated clearly, accurately, and appropriately for different audiences. Support senior leaders in adapting communication style between Vietnamese and English-speaking stakeholders. Review and polish bilingual business materials to ensure professionalism, clarity, and consistency. 5. AI and Productivity Tool Support Support the Commercial Hub in applying technology and AI productivity tools to improve daily workflow efficiency. Use AI productivity tools such as ChatGPT, Claude, or similar platforms to support drafting, summarizing, translation, meeting preparation, and document structuring. Help prepare first drafts of reports, meeting summaries, presentation outlines, and briefing notes using appropriate tools. Support the team in improving productivity through digital tools, templates, and structured workflows. Maintain good judgment when using AI tools, especially regarding confidential or sensitive business information. Demonstrate willingness to learn and adopt new tools that can improve team effectiveness.
Job Requirements: Experience Minimum 3 years of experience in an Executive Assistant, Business Assistant, Commercial Coordinator, Business Support, or similar role. Experience supporting senior leaders or management teams is preferred. Prior experience in a commercial, sales, business development, consulting, professional services, or fast-paced corporate environment is an advantage. Language Strong written and spoken communication skills in both English and Vietnamese. Technical Skills Comfortable working with technology, digital tools, and internal business systems. Good command of Microsoft Office. Experience with CRM systems such as HubSpot, Salesforce, Zoho, Microsoft Dynamics, or similar platforms is a plus. Familiarity with AI productivity tools is preferred. Able to work with reports, dashboards, trackers, and structured data. Education University degree required. Field of study is flexible. Nice to Have Experience working in Vietnamese locally-owned companies , not only multinational corporations. Good understanding of how decisions are made, relationships are managed, and communication flows in a local business environment.

Job Description: Main responsibilities and tasks: Strategic and Policy Coordination: Coordinate strategic and policy advice activities with Vietnamese and international partners, GIZ teams, and consultants in the field of QA and QAM for TVET at macro and meso levels, including: Further developing the TVET Quality Assurance Management System (QAMS) at the national level, including processes and standards. Developing standards and processes for training facilities and equipment management. Conceptualizing the model for colleges serving as national and regional centers for TVET. Preparing policy-level advisory documents, such as policy briefs, reports, and research papers on QA and QAM topics. Capacity Development: Plan, coordinate, and implement capacity development activities to enhance the understanding and implementation of QA and QAM strategies, action plans, and teaching materials for TVET leaders, managers, staff, and teachers. Conduct training courses, workshops, conferences, seminars, webinars, and technical exchanges. Community of Practice (CoP) Development: Facilitate the development and operation of Community of Practice (CoP) activities related to greening and QA with TVET partners Product/Result Dissemination: Coordinate the dissemination of products and results such as models, implementation methods, best practices, technical guidelines, online courses/micro-credentials, etc. at both meso and macro levels. Partnership and Stakeholder Engagement: Foster partnerships between international donors, DVCET/VQA (MOET), TVET colleges, enterprises, and associations to strengthen multi-partner engagement in QA and QAM in TVET. Organize and coordinate technical working group meetings with colleagues, partner TVET colleges, consultants, and relevant stakeholders. Monitoring, Evaluation, and Documentation: Monitor and evaluate assigned activities, ensuring proper documentation of project results, activities, and costs. Ensure appropriate data and knowledge man agement systems are in place. Consultant and Procurement Management: Develop Terms of Reference (ToR) for national and international consultants. Plan, coordinate, monitor, and document consultant missions and their deliverables. Inquire about prices, estimate costs, and prepare necessary documents for service and procurement contracts in line with GIZ regulations. Coordination with GIZ and Other Projects: Cooperate with other GIZ TVET components as well as other GIZ projects to ensure alignment and synergy within the broader Programme “Reform of TVET in Viet Nam III” framework. Perform other duties as assigned by the direct supervisor.
Job Requirements: Minimum requirements: Master's Degree in Education, Quality Assurance, Educational Management, or a closely related field. Specialized training or certification in Quality Assurance (QA) and Quality Management (QM), particularly within the context of TVET or higher education. 7 years of experience in Quality Assurance (QA) and Quality Management (QM) systems within TVET or higher education settings, including working on educational measurement, benchmarking, and standard setting. Experience with accreditation processes for TVET or higher education institutions, including familiarity with national accreditation bodies (e.g., Vietnam's MOET, VQA) and internationally recognized accrediting organizations (e.g., EQAR, ENQA, ACQUIN, ABET). Certified accreditor or demonstrated involvement in accreditation processes, either as an accreditation panel member or through certification from a recognized national or international organization, is an advantage. Experience in preparing policy-level advisory documents, such as policy briefs, recommendation papers, research papers, and strategic plans related to QA/QM for TVET or higher education institutions. Deep understanding of the Vietnamese TVET system, including national policies, frameworks, and the role of institutions under MOET, like the Department of Continuing and Vocational Education and Training (DCVET) and the Vietnam Educational Quality Management Department (VQA). In-depth knowledge of accreditation standards and benchmarking systems for TVET or higher education institutions, and the ability to apply international best practices in the Vietnamese context. In-depth understanding of international QA and QM frameworks, including ISO standards, ENQA guidelines, EQAVET, EHEA, ABET, and Vietnam’s national QA regulations. Strong understanding of educational measurement techniques, assessment systems, and data-driven approaches to improve quality in TVET or higher education. Excellent coordination skills, with a demonstrated ability to work with a variety of stakeholders, including government ministries, international donors, TVET colleges, universities, and private sector partners. Strong facilitation skills for delivering training, workshops, seminars, and capacity-building activities for TVET leaders, managers, and educators. Proficient user of the English language (both spoken and written) Ability to develop and manage Terms of Reference (ToR) for national and international consultants, ensuring high-quality consultancy deliverables and strong project oversight. Ability to foster partnerships with national accreditation bodies, international organizations, TVET institutions, and key stakeholders to drive multi-partner engagement in QA and QM initiatives. Ability to monitor and evaluate complex QA/QM activities, ensuring effective documentation of outcomes, lessons learned, and best practices. Willingness to travel regularly to partner regions and institutions to provide on-the-ground support and ensure quality assurance processes are implemented effectively. Job Description: We are looking for a proactive and experienced Legal & Compliance Specialist to join the Group Legal & Compliance Team, playing a key role in designing and driving a centralized compliance framework across Galaxy Digital Holdings and its subsidiaries. This role primarily focuses on compliance management (70%), ensuring that business operations align with regulatory requirements and internal policies. The role also provides legal support (30%), particularly in commercial and regulatory matters. Responsibilities: Compliance Responsibilities (70%) Design and implement a risk-based compliance framework across the Group, ensuring alignment between centralized policies and business unit operations. Organize and deliver training and awareness programs to embed a compliance culture and ensure employees properly implement internal policies and regulatory requirements. Conduct compliance monitoring and assessments to identify and mitigate potential risks, evaluate adherence to laws and internal policies, and coordinate remediation actions. Stay updated on regulatory developments and assess their operational impact on business activities. Act as a focal point to coordinate with other departments, ensuring consistent compliance practices across the organization. Support regulatory reporting and coordinate responses during inspections or audits as required. Maintain comprehensive compliance documentation on SharePoint according to the company’s document management system and robust audit trails. Legal Responsibilities (30%) Provide legal support on technology-driven business models, including fintech, e-commerce platforms, digital services, and outsourcing structures. Draft, review, and negotiate a wide variety of commercial agreements, including NDAs, IT service agreements, partnership agreements, and outsourcing contracts. Provide legal advice on corporate, commercial, and technology-related matters, ensuring alignment with business strategies. Stay updated on relevant legal and regulatory changes, particularly in areas like data privacy, eKYC, cybersecurity, and consumer protection. Handle other ad hoc legal tasks as assigned by the Line Manager.
Job Requirements: Bachelor’s degree in law from a recognized university; Lawyer Training Certificate is a plus. At least 5 years of legal experience, with a proven track record in corporate compliance management and strong focus on technology, fintech, e-commerce or financial services. In-depth knowledge of Vietnamese laws relating to technology, fintech, ecommerce, data privacy, and commercial contracts. Experience in designing compliance frameworks and working in group-level governance structures is highly preferred. Strong attention to detail, analytical thinking, and problem-solving ability. Fluency in both Vietnamese and English. Positive attitude, responsibility, and ability to work independently or in teams. BENEFITS: Competitive salary package Probation period salary is 100% of the official salary Comprehensive health and accident insurance 15 days of annual leave, 3 days working from home per month Provision of work equipment (Macbook/Laptop, mouse, monitor, etc.) A creative and modern working environment.

Job Description: Company Overview: Coherent Corp. is a global leaders in datacenter and communications industries. Headquartered in Saxonburg, Pennsylvania, USA, and founded in 1971, Coherent operates in over 20 countries worldwide. Since entering Vietnam in 2005, Coherent has expanded to over 2,400 employees across Binh Duong (VSIP1), Dong Nai (Nhon Trach 1), Hanoi, and Bac Ninh (Yen Phong IP). As part of our global growth strategy and commitment to local talent development, we aim to create over 4,000 new job opportunities in Vietnam within the next five years. Business Title: Manufacturing Excellences Manager Work Location: VSIP 1 - BÌNH DƯƠNG BASIC FUNCTION OF THE JOB: - Lead Automation Programs (35%), reducing manual dependency and increasing productivity; - Drive Innovation and Digitalization (35%) to strengthen data visibility and technology adoption; - Enhance Industrial Engineering (20%) through improved process flow, layout efficiency, and cycle-time optimization; - Provide Project Management (10%) for structured cross-functional execution. Automation Development • Develop and execute the automation roadmap from manual to full automation. • Drive automation across grinding, polishing, coating, inspection, and material handling. • Assess processes and build ROI-based automation proposals. • Co-develop scalable automation solutions with Engineering & Production. • Lead end-to-end automation projects from concept to stabilization. • Manage integrators and vendors to ensure safety, quality, and operational fit. • Track post-deployment performance to deliver productivity and quality gains. Innovation, Digitalization & Industry 4.0 • Build and execute a manufacturing digitalization strategy aligned with Coherent global. • Introduce and scale technologies such as: MES / traceability systems, Real-time production dashboards, Automated data collection, AI-based inspection or process monitoring, Predictive maintenance for key optics equipment. • Pilot new digital/automation solutions and scale successful initiatives. Production Industrial Engineering • Optimize manual, semi-automated, and automated processes to improve productivity, quality, and repeatability. • Maintain Standard Work, time studies, line balancing, and capacity models for stable operations and scaling. • Analyze production data to identify bottlenecks and improve cycle time, yield, layout, and material flow. • Support FMEA, root-cause analysis, and corrective actions using 5 Why, RCA, and DMAIC. • Work cross-functionally with Production, Quality, Engineering, and Automation to ensure stable pilot builds, smooth ramp-up, and consistent output. Project Management • Lead strategic projects across Automation, CI, and Digitalization. • Define project scope, charter, timelines, resources, and budgets. • Coordinate cross-functional teams including Production, Quality, Engineering, Maintenance, and IT. • Track milestones, risks, deliverables, and provide executive updates. • Ensure projects meet intended performance outcomes and are delivered on time.
Job Requirements: Education : Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Manufacturing Engineering, Industrial Engineering, Automation, Mechanical/Electrical Engineering, or related fields. Experience: • 8+ years in automation, manufacturing excellence, industrial engineering, continuous improvement and lean mindset, process engineering, or operational excellence. • Strong background in precision manufacturing, optics, electronics, or manual assembly environments. • Proven experience managing cross-functional projects from concept to execution. Competencies • Project management excellence (PMP or equivalent is a plus). • Strong leadership & cross-functional collaboration. • Strategic thinking with hands-on implementation. • Data-driven decision making. • Change management and CI culture building. • Effective communication in English. • Effective communication & ownership. Key Performance Indicators • Automation deployment success & ROI • OEE improvement • Labor productivity & operational efficiency • Scrap reduction & yield improvement • Cost & Efficiency Impact from IE Projects • Digitalization adoption & measurable results • On-time project delivery What We Offer: • Flexible Working Hours: Standard 40-hour workweek with flexible arrangements to support work-life balance and productivity. • Flexible Working Location: Employees are entitled to 6–12 flexible working days per calendar year, depending on the position. • Free Shuttle Service: Staff transportation from Dong Nai and Ho Chi Minh City to our offices in Nhon Trach and VSIP1. • Competitive Compensation Package: Competitive salary with annual reviews, plus allowances for accommodation, transportation, parking. • Attractive Annual Bonus: From 1.96 to 4.00 months of salary, including the 13th-month bonus (effective for new offers from July 2025). • Premium Health Insurance: Comprehensive BVH coverage for employees, with optional family inclusion. • Employee Well-being & Development: Training programs, annual health check-ups, holiday gifts, travel opportunities, sports events, and more. At Coherent, our people are the driving force behind our success. Through continuous improvement in talent acquisition and management, we deliver a seamless and inclusive experience that empowers individuals to thrive. Our commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion ensures every candidate and employee is valued, supported, and positioned to make meaningful contributions to our mission and long-term growth. Join Coherent and become part of a team, today.

Job Description: Macro Strategist - Success Profile The Macro Strategist serves as a key thought leader in interpreting macroeconomic trends, policy shifts, and market dynamics to guide investment strategy and asset allocation. This role requires a blend of analytical rigor, strategic foresight, and communication excellence to influence decision-making across portfolios and client engagements. Responsibilities Macroeconomic Analysis: Monitor, forecast and interpret global and domestic economic indicators, (geo)political developments, and market trends. Investment Strategy Development: Formulate top-down investment theses and translate them into actionable strategies across asset classes (equities, fixed income, real estate, FX, alternatives). Asset allocations: Create strategic asset allocation model portfolio Thought Leadership: Produce high-quality research reports, white papers, presentations for internal stakeholders and clients, and nd media engagements Cross-functional Collaboration: Partner with portfolio managers, asset allocators, and risk teams to align macro views with portfolio construction. Client Engagement: Communicate macro views to clients, contributing to investment committees, roadshows, and strategic reviews.
Job Requirements: Strategic Thinking Ability to synthesize complex macro data into clear, actionable insights. Anticipates market inflection points and policy shifts with a forward-looking lens. Balances long-term strategic views with short-term tactical opportunities. Technical Expertise Deep understanding of macroeconomic theory, global financial systems, and capital markets. Proficiency in econometric modeling, scenario analysis, and forecasting tools. Strong understanding of global and domestic macroeconomics, financial markets, asset classes, and investment products. Good technical/data/system development skills ​Experience & Qualifications 5-10 years experience in macro research and investment strategy Experience working in international asset management, wealth management is a plus Advanced degree in Economics, Finance, or related field (PhD or Master’s preferred). Good at communication, public speaking and presentation skills, with the ability to articulate macro views clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences. Builds credibility with senior stakeholders and clients through insight and conviction Language: compulsory Vietnamese fluent Competencies Visionary investment thinking Strong analytical and quantitative capabilities High ethical standards and fiduciary responsibility

Job Description: MÔ TẢ CÔNG VIỆC: LAVA | 25 FIT là hệ sinh thái Fitness & Wellness hiện đại dành cho thế hệ trẻ yêu thích sự tích cực, năng động và không ngừng nâng cấp bản thân mỗi ngày. Với đa dạng bộ môn như EMS Training, Hot Yoga, Pilates, Cycling, Kickboxing,... chúng tôi không chỉ mang đến trải nghiệm luyện tập, mà còn xây dựng một phong cách sống khỏe mạnh và đầy cảm hứng cho cộng đồng. Chúng tôi đang tìm kiếm một Marketing Manager có tư duy chiến lược, năng lực dẫn dắt đội ngũ và định hướng tăng trưởng thương hiệu trong lĩnh vực Fitness & Lifestyle, sẵn sàng đồng hành cùng thương hiệu trong hành trình mở rộng hệ sinh thái Fitness tiên phong tại Việt Nam. Vị trí này sẽ chịu trách nhiệm xây dựng, quản lý và tối ưu toàn bộ hoạt động Marketing nhằm nâng cao nhận diện thương hiệu, thúc đẩy tăng trưởng khách hàng và gia tăng hiệu quả kinh doanh trên đa kênh — từ chiến lược thương hiệu, các chiến dịch truyền thông đến trải nghiệm khách hàng tại mọi điểm chạm. 1. Hoạch định chiến lược Marketing & Phát triển thương hiệu (Brand + Growth) Định vị và phát triển hình ảnh thương hiệu theo định hướng Fitness – Wellness – Lifestyle hiện đại, khác biệt và phù hợp với khách hàng mục tiêu. Xây dựng kế hoạch Marketing theo tháng/quý/năm gắn với mục tiêu kinh doanh: tăng trưởng lead, booking, conversion và doanh thu. Đề xuất và triển khai các chiến dịch truyền thông nhằm nâng cao nhận diện thương hiệu và mở rộng thị phần. Đảm bảo tính đồng bộ của thương hiệu trên toàn bộ điểm chạm khách hàng (online & offline). 2. Quản lý đội ngũ Marketing (8-12 nhân sự) Quản lý, đào tạo và phát triển đội ngũ Marketing gồm các mảng: Content, Design, Video Production, Brand Partnership/KOL và Event & Campaign. Thiết lập KPI, theo dõi hiệu suất và đánh giá hiệu quả công việc của từng thành viên trong team. Xây dựng quy trình vận hành Marketing chuyên nghiệp, đảm bảo sự phối hợp hiệu quả giữa các bộ phận liên quan. Định hướng tư duy sáng tạo, tinh thần chủ động và văn hóa làm việc hướng kết quả cho đội ngũ. 3. Performance Marketing & Growth Funnel Quản lý và tối ưu hệ thống Marketing funnel: traffic → lead → booking trial → conversion → retention. Triển khai các chiến dịch theo từng mục tiêu kinh doanh như: khai trương studio (launch campaign), chương trình trải nghiệm tập thử (trial campaign), chiến dịch theo mùa (seasonal campaign) và các hoạt động thúc đẩy doanh thu theo từng giai đoạn. Quản lý ngân sách marketing và tối ưu hiệu quả theo chỉ số: CPL, CAC, ROI, booking rate, Conversion Rate và Revenue. Phối hợp với các bộ phận vận hành và sales nhằm nâng cao trải nghiệm khách hàng và tối ưu tỷ lệ chuyển đổi. Theo dõi xu hướng thị trường, hành vi khách hàng và các hoạt động của đối thủ để đề xuất chiến lược tăng trưởng phù hợp. 4. KOL/KOC & Brand Partnership Marketing Xây dựng định hướng chiến lược phát triển mạng lưới KOL/KOC, Influencer và các đối tác thương hiệu phù hợp với định vị và mục tiêu phát triển của LAVA | 25 FIT. Định hướng, giám sát việc triển khai các hoạt động Influencer Marketing, Brand Partnership, Co-branding và Activation nhằm gia tăng độ phủ thương hiệu, thu hút khách hàng tiềm năng và hỗ trợ mục tiêu kinh doanh. Theo dõi, đánh giá hiệu quả đầu tư truyền thông (ROI) của các hoạt động KOL/KOC và hợp tác thương hiệu; Đưa ra các đề xuất tối ưu dựa trên dữ liệu và báo cáo từ đội ngũ phụ trách 5. Báo cáo & Tối ưu hiệu quả (Data-driven Marketing) Theo dõi, phân tích và đánh giá hiệu quả hoạt động Marketing theo tuần/tháng/quý. Phân tích dữ liệu campaign và đưa ra insight tối ưu chiến lược Marketing . Báo cáo trực tiếp BOD về hiệu quả: marketing performance + business impact. Đề xuất cải thiện liên tục về: content, kênh, ngân sách, hiệu suất chuyển đổi. QUYỀN LỢI: Thu nhập: Lương cạnh tranh cao: 35-60 triệu (thương lượng theo năng lực) Phúc lợi: 365 ngày trải nghiệm miễn phí hệ sinh thái luyện tập tại LAVA | 25 FIT Trực tiếp tham gia xây dựng và phát triển thương hiệu Fitness & Wellness đang tăng trưởng mạnh tại Việt Nam. Môi trường năng động, sáng tạo, định hướng hiệu suất và phát triển. Cơ hội phát triển lên Head of Marketing / Marketing Director trong tương lai. Thưởng cuối năm + thưởng hiệu suất theo kết quả kinh doanh. Có HĐLĐ; Được đóng BHXH và đầy đủ BHYT/BHTN theo Luật Lao động quy định. Chương trình khen thưởng nội bộ. Và các quyền lợi, chính sách phúc lợi khác theo Luật lao động quy định.
Job Requirements: Tối thiểu 4 - 5 năm kinh nghiệm thực chiến trong lĩnh vực Marketing, trong đó có ít nhất 2 năm ở vị trí quản lý đội ngũ từ 5 người trở lên. Ưu tiên ứng viên có kinh nghiệm trong các ngành: Fitness, Wellness, Beauty, FMCG, Retail, Lifestyle hoặc Agency. Có kinh nghiệm triển khai đồng thời brand marketing + performance marketing . Có tư duy tài chính tốt: Thành thạo việc lập kế hoạch, phân bố và tối ưu hóa ngân sách Marketing (Marketing Budget). Chịu trách nhiệm trực tiếp về hiệu quả chi phí trên từng mục tiêu (CPL, CAC, ROI) . Hiểu rõ data-driven, hiểu rõ funnel marketing & customer journey và tư duy tăng trưởng (Growth mindset). Kỹ năng leadership, stakeholder management tốt. Chủ động, sáng tạo, có tinh thần ownership cao và khả năng làm việc trong môi trường tăng trưởng nhanh. Ưu tiên ứng viên yêu thích thể thao, sức khỏe và phong cách sống năng động.

Job Title: Kiểm toán nội bộ, Hội sở - Kiểm toán viên (phụ trách mô hình, dữ liệu)
Company: Ngân Hàng TMCP Phương Đông - OCB
Job Description: 1. Kiểm toán mô hình (Model Audit) - Đánh giá tính phù hợp của phương pháp luận xây dựng mô hình (model development) theo chuẩn mực NHNN, Basel II/III, IFRS 9 và quy định nội bộ. - Kiểm tra tính chính xác của dữ liệu đầu vào, quy trình làm sạch dữ liệu (data cleaning), trích xuất, phân tầng và biến đầu vào của mô hình. - Rà soát thuật toán xây dựng mô hình, logic chấm điểm/ước tính PD/LGD/EAD, threshold cut-off, calibration, validation. - Đánh giá model governance: phê duyệt, vòng đời mô hình, theo dõi và tái kiểm định định kỳ. 2. Kiểm toán mô hình xếp hạng tín dụng (Doanh nghiệp – Cá nhân) - Kiểm tra thiết kế mô hình, trọng số biến, phương pháp tính điểm, tiêu chí xếp hạng & mapping sang nhóm nợ. - Đánh giá tính ổn định mô hình, mức độ phân biệt (discriminatory power), tính dự báo (predictive power), back–testing. 3. Kiểm toán mô hình CAR & ICAAP - Rà soát phương pháp tính CAR, phân bổ tài sản có rủi ro (RWA), trọng số rủi ro, dữ liệu đầu vào. - Kiểm toán quy trình ICAAP: nhận diện rủi ro, đo lường, stress test, kịch bản, phân bổ vốn nội bộ và báo cáo cho HĐQT/NHNN. 4. Phân tích dữ liệu phục vụ kiểm toán - Thiết kế và chạy các bộ test dữ liệu: tính toàn vẹn dữ liệu, logic nghiệp vụ, so khớp chéo giữa các hệ thống. - Xây dựng bộ chỉ số cảnh báo sớm (early warning), hỗ trợ phát hiện sai lệch trong mô hình hoặc dữ liệu. - Tự động hóa kiểm toán bằng Python/SQL/Power BI. 5. Báo cáo & Kiến nghị - Lập báo cáo kiểm toán về mô hình, đưa ra khuyến nghị mang tính cải thiện hệ thống, kiểm soát và tuân thủ. - Theo dõi việc khắc phục của đơn vị được kiểm toán.
Job Requirements: 1. Trình độ - Tốt nghiệp đại học trở lên các ngành: Toán thống kê, Kinh tế lượng, Phân tích dữ liệu, Tài chính – Ngân hàng, Rủi ro, Khoa học dữ liệu… - Ưu tiên ứng viên có các chứng chỉ: FRM, CFA, Data Analyst, Machine Learning, Basel/IFRS 9, hoặc kinh nghiệm tại Big4, ngân hàng. 2. Kiến thức chuyên môn cần có - Hiểu biết sâu về mô hình xếp hạng tín dụng, mô hình IRB/Basel II–III, mô hình stress test, ICAAP. - Kiến thức vững về nghiệp vụ ngân hàng, tín dụng, rủi ro tín dụng, quản lý mô hình (model governance). - Am hiểu phương pháp luận xây dựng & kiểm định mô hình: - Logistic regression, scorecard, machine learning căn bản - Gini, KS, ROC, stability index, calibration - Nắm vững điều kiện dữ liệu: chất lượng dữ liệu, lineage, cleansing, transformation. 3. Kỹ năng kỹ thuật - Thành thạo một trong các công cụ: - SQL, Python (pandas, numpy, sklearn), R, Power BI, SAS, Excel nâng cao. - Kỹ năng đọc hiểu tài liệu mô hình, đặc tả kỹ thuật và tài liệu phương pháp luận. - Kỹ năng xử lý tập dữ liệu lớn, kiểm thử dữ liệu, phát hiện sai lệch. 4. Kinh nghiệm - Tối thiểu 2–3 năm kinh nghiệm trong một trong các mảng: Phân tích dữ liệu ngân hàng; Xây dựng/kiểm định mô hình; Kiểm toán nội bộ/kiểm toán mô hình; Rủi ro tín dụng, phân tích danh mục. - Ưu tiên ứng viên từng làm tại khối RRTD, Khối Mô hình, Khối QLRR, Big4 Advisory/Analytics. 5. Kỹ năng mềm - Tư duy phân tích – logic xuất sắc - Có khả năng đọc – hiểu hệ thống nhanh - Viết báo cáo rõ ràng, mạch lạc - Giao tiếp tốt với các khối mô hình/QLRR/IT - Tinh thần độc lập, khách quan, tuân thủ cao 6. Tố chất phù hợp - Cẩn trọng, nguyên tắc, trung thực - Không ngại đào sâu dữ liệu phức tạp - Có góc nhìn rủi ro và hiểu bản chất hoạt động ngân hàng - Khả năng làm việc dưới áp lực và deadline kiểm toán

Job Description: The Cost Controller Specialist ensures Ly Gia Vien’s financial data moves beyond compliance into control and insight. This role owns cost transparency, margin accuracy, and allocation discipline across brands, channels, and divisions — building the foundation for controlling and profitability analysis within SAP S/4HANA CO. The goal is to support business decisions with clear financial insights and improve margin performance company-wide. Cost Monitoring & Control : - Track and analyze actual vs. standard cost for materials, freight, duties, and warehouse handling. - Investigate cost variances by brand, supplier, or shipment. - Coordinate with Procurement & Supply Chain on landed cost accuracy and logistics efficiency. - Monitor demo/sample/promotional cost usage and tie it to marketing ROI. Profitability & Margin Analysis : - Prepare Contribution Margin Ladder (Gross to CM3) monthly by brand, product line, and channel (HORECA, GT, MT, E-commerce). - Identify margin dilution drivers (freight, duty, discount, handling, wastage) and recommend actions. - Provide SKU-level profitability data to guide pricing and promotions. - Analyze product mix shifts and margin impact. Cost Center & Expense Management: - Review cost center/profit center postings for accuracy. - Support department heads in analyzing controllable expenses and optimizing spend. - Collaborate with HR to allocate labor costs by department using statistical keys (e.g., pallets, m², FTE). - Maintain and update allocation methods quarterly. Inventory & COGS Reconciliation: - Partner with Inventory Accountant for reconciliation of COGS, stock valuation, and write-offs. - Assess inventory holding cost and Days on Hand (DOH) by category; flag slow-movers. - Track demo/leasing stock costs to ensure correct capitalization or expensing. - Recommend waste reduction and inventory turnover improvements. Reporting & Insights: - Deliver monthly Operational Cost & Margin Report to COO and leadership. - Perform ad-hoc analysis (e.g., supplier cost shifts, freight changes). - Build Excel/Power BI dashboards for decision-making support. - Convert financial data into actionable business insights. Continuous Improvement & Education: - Maintain SOPs for costing, variance analysis, and month-end closing. - Train accounting and operational staff on cost awareness and implications. - Partner with IT/ERP teams to improve data integrity and reporting efficiency. - Participate in QBRs to integrate finance with planning.
Job Requirements: Experience 03+ years in costing/controlling (FMCG, distribution, or manufacturing preferred). Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, Industrial Engineering, or related field. Proficient in SAP CO or equivalent ERP module (SAP S/4HANA preferred). Advanced Excel and Power BI skills. Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite. Understanding of or interest in the culinary, hospitality, or foodservice equipment sector. Strong analytical mindset and process-oriented thinker. Excellent organizational, analytical skills
Industry: Hình thức Nhân viên chính thức

Job Description: The General Accounting Supervisor is responsible for ensuring accurate and timely financial statements & tax reports, maintaining a secure and comprehensive documentation system. The requirements include a minimum of 3 years of general accounting experience, strong technical knowledge of accounting standards (VAS, IFRS), and proficiency in using leading accounting software like Oracle and SAP. Job Responsibility Ensure that financial statements comply with Vietnamese Accounting Standards, International Accounting Standards, tax regulations, and the company's Chart of Accounts (CoA). Conduct periodic closings of the General Ledger (GL) module by monitoring accounting transactions, performing entries, and preparing timely financial statements. Ensure all journal entries are supported by detailed, clear, and valid documentation. Reconcile and verify audit reports before issuance, adhering to stipulated deadlines. Provide input to optimize the ERP system for financial reporting and review processes. Maintain a thorough understanding of tax decrees and circulars to ensure correct application and optimization of tax regulations in report preparation. Prepare tax reports within deadlines and assist the tax advisory department in finalization data, providing explanations to the tax audit team as needed. Systematically and securely store data, records, and legal documents as required by law, including company legal files, contracts, financial statements, audit reports, and tax reports. Organize data storage in a scientific manner to support various reporting needs and ensure comprehensive access on network drives. Prepare specialized reports for investors, banks, and other authorities, and participate in periodic inventory checks as per company regulations. A minimum of three years of general accounting experience is required, along with a strong attention to detail.
Job Requirements: MS Office Business English Accounting standards knowledge: VAS & IFRS Accounting systems knowledge (Oracle, SAP) Functional Competencies Accounting information systems Risk management and internal control Education Bachelors in Business Administration/ Accounting
Experience: Cấp bậc Trưởng nhóm / Giám sát

Job Title: Giám đốc Giải pháp Phi Tín Dụng (Head of Non-Lending Solution) - TNEX - 4B046
Company: CÔNG TY TÀI CHÍNH TNHH MTV TNEX (TNEX Finance)
Job Description: Mục tiêu vị trí Chịu trách nhiệm toàn diện về chiến lược, tăng trưởng kinh doanh và P&L của mảng thẻ tín dụng tại ngân hàng số. Dẫn dắt end-to-end card business bao gồm acquisition, activation, spend growth, revolving balance, portfolio profitability, risk alignment và customer experience nhằm xây dựng mảng thẻ tín dụng tăng trưởng bền vững và có lợi nhuận. Vị trí này đóng vai trò lãnh đạo xuyên suốt giữa Business, Product, Risk, Finance, Marketing, Technology và Operations để xây dựng một hệ sinh thái thẻ tín dụng cạnh tranh trên thị trường. Trách nhiệm chính 1) Quản lý P&L & chiến lược kinh doanh thẻ • Chịu trách nhiệm trực tiếp về P&L của toàn bộ business thẻ tín dụng. • Xây dựng chiến lược tăng trưởng và profitability cho portfolio thẻ tín dụng theo từng giai đoạn. • Thiết lập business model, pricing strategy và customer segmentation phù hợp với định hướng ngân hàng số. • Quản lý và tối ưu các nguồn doanh thu: o Interchange income o Interest income o Annual fee o Installment fee o Merchant partnership revenue o Cross-sell/upsell revenue • Quản lý và tối ưu cost structure: o Acquisition cost o Reward/cashback cost o Funding cost o Fraud loss o Credit loss o Operational cost 2) Tăng trưởng khách hàng & chi tiêu thẻ • Xây dựng chiến lược acquisition, activation và retention khách hàng thẻ tín dụng. • Dẫn dắt các chiến lược thúc đẩy: o Spend volume o Spend per active card o Revolving balance o Customer engagement • Phát triển merchant ecosystem và partnership strategy để gia tăng transaction volume. • Xây dựng loyalty/rewards ecosystem phù hợp với customer segments và unit economics. • Theo dõi hành vi khách hàng và market trends để tối ưu product-market fit. 3) Product & Portfolio Management • Định hướng product roadmap cho toàn bộ sản phẩm thẻ tín dụng. • Phê duyệt và đánh giá business case cho các sáng kiến sản phẩm, tính năng và campaign mới. • Đảm bảo portfolio phát triển cân bằng giữa: o Growth o Profitability o Risk o Customer experience • Theo dõi hiệu quả danh mục thẻ theo từng phân khúc khách hàng và từng cohort. 4) Risk & Portfolio Quality Management • Phối hợp chặt chẽ với Risk team để xây dựng: o Credit policy o Underwriting strategy o Limit assignment o Collection strategy • Theo dõi và kiểm soát: o Delinquency o NPL o Fraud rate o Vintage performance o Portfolio health • Đảm bảo tăng trưởng business đi cùng với kiểm soát rủi ro bền vững. 5) Technology, Operations & Card Lifecycle Management • Làm việc chặt chẽ với Engineering và Architecture teams để xây dựng scalable card platform. • Định hướng các năng lực công nghệ liên quan: o Card issuing & processing o API ecosystem o Real-time decisioning o Rewards engine o Fraud prevention o Data & analytics infrastructure • Làm việc với các đối tác như processor, payment gateway, merchant network và card schemes như Visa và Mastercard. • Đảm bảo vận hành ổn định và tối ưu customer journey end-to-end. 6) Leadership & Cross-functional Management • Xây dựng và dẫn dắt team Credit Card Business bao gồm: o Product o Growth o Portfolio management o Partnership o Business analytics • Thiết lập KPI và vận hành business review định kỳ. • Điều phối hiệu quả giữa Business, Risk, Finance, Technology, Marketing và Operations. • Xây dựng văn hóa data-driven và customer-centric trong team.
Job Requirements: Tốt nghiệp từ Đại học, các chuyên ngành về Kinh tế, IT hoặc liên quan. • Từ 8–12 năm kinh nghiệm trong: o Credit card business o Consumer lending o Retail banking o Digital banking o Fintech • Có ít nhất 3–5 năm kinh nghiệm quản lý team hoặc business unit. • Ưu tiên ứng viên từng chịu trách nhiệm trực tiếp về: o Card P&L o Portfolio growth o Revolving business o Card profitability Kiến thức chuyên môn Business & Finance • Hiểu sâu về unit economics và business model của thẻ tín dụng. • Có kinh nghiệm quản lý P&L và xây dựng chiến lược profitability. • Thành thạo business case evaluation, forecasting và portfolio planning. Credit & Risk • Hiểu rõ: o Credit lifecycle o Underwriting o Fraud management o Collections o Risk-adjusted return • Có khả năng cân bằng giữa growth và portfolio quality. Product, Technology & Operations • Có hiểu biết tốt về: o Card systems o Payment ecosystem o API integrations o Mobile banking/digital banking architecture o Agile product delivery o Card operations & dispute processes • Có khả năng làm việc hiệu quả với Engineering, Operations và Data teams. • Có tư duy tối ưu vận hành và cải thiện customer experience trên quy mô lớn. Leadership • Strong strategic thinking và execution capability. • Kỹ năng lãnh đạo, stakeholder management và influencing mạnh. • Có khả năng vận hành trong môi trường tốc độ cao và nhiều thay đổi. QUYỀN LỢI KHI GIA NHẬP TNEX FINANCE 1. Lương thưởng hấp dẫn: • Mức lương cạnh tranh so với thị trường và tương xứng với năng lực, sẽ được thương lượng khi ứng viên vượt qua vòng Phỏng vấn. • Thưởng hiệu suất cuối năm từ 2 đến 5 tháng lương trung bình của năm, tùy theo kết quả đánh giá xếp loại. 2. Chính sách phúc lợi toàn diện: • Bảo hiểm theo quy định pháp luật và bảo hiểm sức khỏe riêng cho cán bộ nhân viên. • Các chế độ phúc lợi đa dạng: giờ làm việc linh hoạt, nghỉ mát, nghỉ sinh nhật … • Có chương trình vay với lãi suất ưu đãi cho CBNV. 3. Đào tạo và thăng tiến: • Chương trình Đào tạo chuyên sâu, bài bản theo hướng chuyển đổi (Re_skill, Up_skill, New_skill), được biên tập tùy theo từng nhóm vị trí công việc. • Được hướng dẫn và kèm cặp tận tình bởi các lãnh đạo, quản lý trực tiếp và chuyên gia trong ngành. • Chương trình phát triển quản lý dành cho các nhân viên có tiềm năng, giúp họ chuẩn bị sẵn sàng cho các vị trí quản lý và lãnh đạo trong tương lai. • Ưu tiên tuyển dụng cấp quản lý từ nhân sự nội bộ, tạo cơ hội thăng tiến cho nhân viên. 4. Môi trường làm việc chuyên nghiệp và văn hóa doanh nghiệp năng động: • Thoải mái, thân thiện, đề cao tinh thần trách nhiệm, hỗ trợ lẫn nhau. • Năng động, sáng tạo, khuyến khích đổi mới và học hỏi liên tục. • Tôn trọng sự khác biệt, đề cao tinh thần làm việc nhóm và hợp tác hiệu quả. • Nhiều hoạt động văn hóa, thể thao, giải trí sôi nổi, tạo môi trường làm việc gắn kết và vui vẻ.
Industry: Ngân hàng

Company: TAVN
Job Description: Job Overview: The Pricing Officer is responsible for managing the pricing ticketing system, ensuring the timely resolution of pricing issues, and providing critical support across various departments. This role includes handling and assigning tickets, resolving pricing requests, addressing pricing holds, and processing customer claims. The Pricing Specialist will also upload pricing into JDE and ensure its accurate configuration. The Pricing Specialist will also assist in developing SOPs and provide support for other pricing-related projects. This hybrid role will require an office presence for three to four days on-site at our Houston Spenwick facility during regular business hours. RectorSeal Truaire Viet Nam manufactures and/or distributes various chemical and mechanical products for the HVAC, electrical, and plumbing industries. For over 80 years, RectorSeal’s customers have depended on us for quality products with excellent value shipped to them promptly. This passion for exceptional customer service is the reason RectorSeal has been growing at a swift pace. Key Responsibilities: Manage the Pricing ticketing system by promptly responding to and assigning tickets to the appropriate pricing team members. Ensure assigned tickets are resolved within 24 to 48 hours, adhering to established timelines and service level agreements. Process and resolve pricing hold by identifying the root cause and implementing appropriate corrective actions to ensure timely resolution. Maintain a biweekly upload of approved pricing from the Pricing Authorization Request (PAR) system into JDE. Ensure pricing is accurately configured in JDE and promptly address and resolve any issues as they arise. Manage and update external claim back quotes by adding contractors and adjusting pricing as requested by the sales team. Process customer monthly claims and ensure credits are issued within two weeks of submission. Provide pricing support across various departments and teams to ensure a seamless order fulfillment process and efficient communication. Help develop Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) to document critical pricing processes, support onboarding new team members, and standardize pricing procedures and practices. Support pricing-related projects and tasks as requested by the Pricing Manager or Pricing Supervisor, including, but not limited to, price increases, pricing analysis, account setups, product launches, mergers and acquisitions (M&A), JDE integrations, etc.… Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) Average ticket first response and resolution time Average pricing hold resolution time Average claim processing time Average approved pricing upload time
Job Requirements: Required Experience: High School Diploma or equivalent 1+ Years of experience in pricing or customer service Hands-on experience with ticketing systems Proficient with JD Edwards or other Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems Skills and Competencies Excellent problem-solving and organizational skills Ability to manage multiple tasks and prioritize effectively Proficient in data analysis and reporting tools Customer-focused and continuous improvement mindset with attention to detail Preferred Requirements: Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business Administration or related degree
Industry: Hình thức Nhân viên chính thức

Company: LOTTE VIETNAM SHOPPING JOINT STOCK COMPANY
Welfare: ['Chế độ bảo hiểm', 'Du Lịch', 'Chế độ thưởng']
Job Description: Mission 1: Creating a product development plan in charge of all store: Surveying the market, competitors and updating new trends. Developing plans, purchasing new products and exploiting specific product groups. Analyzing the business situation of the category in charge at each store to build promotions and develop the category to ensure sales and profits. Collaborating with the direct managers, colleagues, supervisors of the Fashion Department and directors at store on the plan to determine the tasks that need to be done Mission 2: Implementing the set plans for effective product development and management: Surveying, selecting and sourcing new products in line with market trends and consumer tastes. Negotiating prices with suppliers. Working with QA team to support quality inspection of items to ensure business products comply with regulations on product quality and labels of the Ministry of Trade & Industry. Preparing and ensuring purchase and sale contracts, commercial agreements, lease contracts, etc. with suppliers are implemented in accordance with the company and the state regulations with support from superiors (if necessary). Providing information forms about new suppliers, new products to generate supplier codes, product codes on the system and notifying to relevant departments: MD planning, Marketing Department, stores and vendors. Tracking and managing the performance of defined item categories in terms of price, promotion, product assortment and product quality. Planning and allocating each item to each store. Ensuring that the pricing strategy is fully applied and understood by the Suppliers. Maintaining effective internal communication channels, marketing, operations, sales and marketing to attract customers. Monitoring the sales of the category on a daily, monthly, and quarterly basis to promptly offer solutions to promote sales to achieve the set goals. Mission 3:Checking the implementation progress of the plans for the categoy: Monitoring the progress of the plan implementation: launching new products, changing the display arrangement, etc. Cross-checking between store staff and suppliers' PG to understand the business situation of the category at the stores. Solving arising problems (if any) so that the plan is implemented on time. Mission 4: Reporting the results of the implementation of the plan to superiors: Reporting all related issues to the direct manager; Making weekly and monthly reports on the tasks in charge for HOD and GD; Reporting the annual plan. Other: Other tasks at the request of direct manager, general director. Going on a business trip when there is a need to change the display at the warehouse or open a new store.
Job Requirements: Education level : College or above. Major : Economics, fashion design and related professions Expertise: Knowledge of the retail industry (supermarkets, chain stores) Knowledge of fashion. Experience in purchasing goods. Necessary skills: Good communication skills Good negotiation skills Reporting and work organization skills Proficient in Excel and Power Point Basic English Other special requirements: Be honest. Be disciplined and work independently, good at collaborating people, withstand pressure. Ability to travel for job and work overtime. Knowing how to use GMD data management software is an advantage. Specific professional experience: Minimum 2 years of experience working in supermarkets, retail chains. Minimum 3 years of experience in the field of purchasing goods.
Industry: Hình thức Nhân viên chính thức

Provide guidance and consultation regarding: Labor Law/Social Insurance/Company policies/Employee relations matters. Culture & Employee Engagement (10%): Coordinate with relevant departments to implement employee engagement, internal communication and corporate culture initiatives. Promote company values and contribute to a positive, collaborative and high-performance workplace culture. Propose initiatives to enhance employee experience and workplace engagement. 4. HR Analytics & Process Improvement (10%) Track, analyze and prepare HR reports to support workforce planning and management decision-making. Participate in HR transformation, HR technology, process standardization or AI adoption initiatives when required BENEFITS: Dynamic and friendly working environment with strong opportunities for personal growth and career advancement. Solid understanding of Vietnam Labor Law/Social Insurance regulations 2. Skills & Competencies: Strong recruitment execution capability. Company: Wanek Furniture
Job Description: The Senior GL Accountant is responsible for bank transactions, Accounts Receivable (AR), VAT output, sales reporting, and intercompany reconciliation. This role ensures accurate, timely, and compliant financial records in line with Vietnam Accounting Standards (VAS), tax regulations, and company policies, while supporting management reporting and statutory requirements. Bank & Cash Management Record daily bank transactions accurately in the accounting system. Perform monthly bank reconciliation, investigate discrepancies, and ensure timely resolution. Coordinate with banks and internal teams regarding payment issues or adjustments. Accounts Receivable (AR) & Sales Reporting Prepare and monitor Accounts Receivable (AR) forecasts to support cash flow planning. Track customer payment status and follow up on overdue balances in coordination with relevant departments. Prepare sales reports, including overseas sales reports, ensuring accuracy and completeness. Perform AR reconciliation related to sales and customer accounts. Intercompany Accounting Perform intercompany reconciliation, ensuring balances between entities are accurate and properly recorded. Investigate and resolve intercompany differences in a timely manner. VAT Output & Tax Compliance Issue VAT output invoices in strict compliance with Vietnamese tax regulations. Prepare VAT output reports based on issued invoices. Support monthly/quarterly VAT declaration and provide explanations or supporting documents when required. Period-End Closing & Reporting Support month-end and year-end closing activities as assigned by the GL Supervisor. Ensure proper accounting documentation is maintained for audit and compliance purposes. Coordination & Support Coordinate with internal departments and external parties to resolve issues related to bank, AR, VAT, and intercompany transactions. Perform other accounting tasks as assigned by management.
Job Requirements: Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related fields. Minimum 3 years of experience in Accounting, Finance, GL, AR/AP , with experience in Manufacturing is an advantage. Good understanding of Vietnam Accounting Standards (VAS), VAT regulations, banking transactions, AR/AP, and intercompany accounting. Familiar with ERP/accounting systems (e.g. SAP, AS400) and proficient in Excel (formulas, pivot tables, data analysis). Strong accounting, reconciliation, reporting, and problem-solving skills. Good communication, coordination, time management, and ability to work independently or in a team. Detail-oriented with the ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines. Intermediate English proficiency (Basic verbal communication and good written skills)
Industry: Kế toán / Kiểm toán , Sản xuất / Vận hành sản xuất , Nội ngoại thất

Quản trị toàn bộ core systems: OMS, RMS, back-office, kết nối KRX/HOSE/HNX, thanh toán bù trừ, market data, kết nối liên ngân hàng trong hệ sinh thái HDBank–Sovico. Dẫn dắt Phát triển sản phẩm: AI advisor, smart trading bot, bảng giá thế hệ mới, ứng dụng mobile/web cho retail và institutional, sản phẩm Block Trade + Allocation Engine, Copy Trade. Phụ trách Vận hành Giao dịch: đảm bảo uptime, latency, năng lực xử lý phiên đỉnh, ATTT theo chuẩn TCTD và yêu cầu UBCKNN. Quản trị hạ tầng (cloud/on-prem hybrid), data platform, an ninh mạng, DR/BCP. Quyết định build-vs-buy; quản lý đối tác công nghệ chiến lược (KRX core vendor, fintech partners, hyperscaler). Chuẩn bị nền tảng công nghệ đáp ứng yêu cầu IPO/niêm yết HOSE và mở rộng tham gia VIFC.
Job Requirements: Yêu cầu Tối thiểu 15 năm trong fintech hoặc securities/banking technology, có kinh nghiệm vận hành core trading system quy mô ≥ 1 triệu khách hàng hoạt động. Am hiểu cấu trúc thị trường chứng khoán VN, chuẩn KRX, khung pháp lý ATTT cho định chế tài chính (Thông tư 09, Nghị định 13, ISO 27001). Đã quản lý đội kỹ thuật ≥ 50 người với tổ chức tách bạch Engineering – Product – Design – SRE/Operations. Ưu tiên ứng viên đến từ CTCK top, ngân hàng số, hoặc fintech khu vực (Sea Group, Ant, Grab Financial, Futu, KakaoBank, các bank-tech Hàn/Sing/Indo). Tư duy sản phẩm mạnh, từng dẫn dắt build platform riêng thay vì chỉ vận hành hệ thống mua sẵn. Tiếng Anh thành thạo; chấp nhận cường độ làm việc cao giai đoạn pre-IPO.

Company: CÔNG TY CỔ PHẦN UPPINGHAM SCHOOL VIỆT NAM - HƯNG YÊN
Job Description: Position Objective: The Senior Purchasing Officer is responsible for executing the school’s operational procurement activities, ensuring the timely, cost-effective, and high-quality provision of educational supplies, stationery, classroom materials, equipment, and services for the school and its affiliated units. This role plays a critical part in supporting teaching and learning activities as well as school operations, while strictly complying with regulations, maintaining cost control, and effectively managing supplier relationships in accordance with the company’s policies and procedures. Key Responsibilities: 1. Purchasing Execution Perform and support the Procurement Manager in the end-to-end procurement process (sourcing, purchase order placement, order follow-up, goods receipt, quality inspection, and payment processing) for educational supplies, furniture, equipment, and other operational needs of the school. Conduct goods inspection, acceptance, and coordinate delivery/handover to the requesting departments. Liaise with suppliers to ensure on-time delivery and promptly resolve quality issues. 2. Sourcing & Supplier Management Perform and support the Procurement Manager in sourcing, evaluating, and maintaining a portfolio of reliable domestic and international suppliers. Participate in negotiating pricing, payment terms, delivery schedules, and warranty conditions with suppliers. 3. Cost Control & Budget Management Ensure all purchases provide the best value for money while meeting quality standards. Track and report cost savings on a regular basis. Collaborate with relevant departments to manage inventory levels and avoid overstocking or shortages. 4. Compliance & Reporting Ensure strict compliance with the company’s internal procurement policies, Vietnamese procurement laws, tax regulations, import/export procedures, and other legal requirements. Maintain accurate procurement records and documentation. Monitor, consolidate, and regularly report procurement progress, actual costs, and order status to the Procurement Manager and relevant departments. Support internal audits and tax inspections related to procurement activities. 5. Collaboration & Support Work closely with user departments to understand procurement needs, technical specifications, and quality requirements. Provide product consultation and recommendations prior to purchase approval. Update order status and delivery timelines to relevant departments in a timely manner. Assist the Procurement Manager in meetings with suppliers and internal stakeholders as required. 6. Other Tasks as Assigned Carry out any other duties and ad-hoc tasks as assigned by the Procurement Manager to support the overall procurement function and school operations.
Job Requirements: Position requirements: Bachelor’s degree in business administration, Supply Chain Management, Economics, Logistics, or related field. Minimum 2-3 years of relevant experience, with preference given to candidates from education sector. Strong understanding of Vietnamese procurement regulations and import procedures. Demonstrated high sense of responsibility, proactivity, diligence, and absolute honesty and integrity throughout the procurement process. Abilities/ Skills: Fluent in both spoken and written Vietnamese and English (mandatory). Strong negotiation, communication, and interpersonal skills. Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications (particularly Excel) and internal procurement/ERP systems. Solid skills in sourcing, data analysis, and report preparation. Excellent teamwork and cross-departmental coordination abilities. High sense of responsibility, proactivity, and integrity.
Experience: Cấp bậc Trưởng nhóm / Giám sát
Industry: Hình thức Nhân viên chính thức
